title: String Hunt
description: A binary `nexus_agent` was found on the CHIMERA node. Running `strings` on it reveals hardcoded credentials. The password is embedded as a sequence of ASCII chars. Locate the flag string which follows the pattern `FLAG{...}`.
hint: Use `strings nexus_agent | grep FLAG` to extract all readable string sequences and filter for the flag pattern.

title: Disassembly Novice
description: A function in `chimera_core.elf` checks a password character by character. The disassembly shows `cmp eax, 0x4e657875` at the comparison. What 4-byte string is being compared? (Assume little-endian x86)
hint: In little-endian x86, bytes are stored in reverse order. Convert hex 0x4e657875 to ASCII bytes reversed: 75='u', 78='x', 65='e', 4e='N'.

title: Anti-Debug Bypass
description: A protected binary calls `ptrace(PTRACE_TRACEME, 0, 0, 0)` at startup to detect debuggers. If ptrace returns -1, it exits. What technique or tool option would you use to bypass this anti-debugging check in GDB?
hint: GDB has built-in mechanisms to handle ptrace-based anti-debugging. Look at how to intercept the ptrace syscall or use the `catch syscall ptrace` command.

title: Packed Binary
description: The `nexus_payload.exe` binary is packed with UPX. The entry point jumps to a decompression stub before executing the real code. What command unpacks a UPX-compressed binary in place?
hint: UPX is a popular executable packer with its own unpacking capability. The `-d` flag performs decompression.
